summary: init() in db.pg.ts creates a Pool with no cleanup path exposed to callers
details: The `init()` function creates a `new Pool(...)` (line 18) for remote Postgres connections but provides no mechanism for callers to close/end the pool. The only way to clean up is `(db as any).$client.end()` — a fragile duck-type cast that's undocumented. This discovery was made during the PG-002 migration runner review; the migration runner needs pool cleanup but cannot get it cleanly. Consider either (a) adding `end()` to `PgClient` type, (b) returning `{ client, end }` from `init()`, or (c) documenting the duck-type pattern.
